generatedSentence: Our protagonist, a `historian` dismissed for his `dorkiness`, lived a `rustic` life far from the `luxury` of the `globe`. His `hobby` was `photography`, capturing the `crumbling` remains of an old `refinery`, its `circuits` and pipes hinting at a forgotten `darkness`. He often felt a `prickly` `heartache` for a `granny` he'd lost, finding scant `entertainment` in a `magazine` detailing ancient `voyageur` tales. His `bandwidth` for social interaction was low, making him a `weirdo`, yet he `awaken`ed daily to the `gleam` of dawn, preparing a `healthy` portion of `ramen` or `noodles`, wearing a `knitted` `jacket` in his `place` that felt like `nowhere` special. One day, he was `hired` by a clandestine organization, their `agenda` `darkly` hinted at a series of `killings` and `murder` under the guise of `layoffs` and `groundings` in a hidden `pentagon` facility. He had to `stand up` to his fears, accepting the mission with a grim `point` of his `pinky` finger. The `adversity` was clear: to bypass a formidable `firewall` and expose the `tricksters` orchestrating the `million` `litre` `drugs` trade that offered only `painkiller` relief to the `hillbilly` populace. He knew this `game` would `hurt` and required him to `justify` his dangerous `intentions`, ignoring the strange `hickeys` on his neck. His mission `awoken` deep `urgencies`; he encountered `vampires` in the `dank` shadows, their `glower` a terrifying `blaze`. He had to `vouchsafe` the truth, using a concealed `flamethrower` to clear the path, its `final` `jingle` of ignition echoing in the tunnels. He fought with `karate` precision, a `batman`-like figure wielding an `umbrella` against the `milky` `vapours` of their enchantments. The `ruler` of this syndicate, a `tribalistic` figure, `indicts` him with a `stone`-cold `thumbs`-down, signaling the `final` `farewell` of any hope. He observed his foe bidding `oysters` and `nectarine` to his `parties`, a chilling display of `luxury` amidst the `earthquake` of discovery. Through the collapsing `door`, amidst the `icing` `flurry` of battle, our `historian` `walk away` from the `warship` once he secured the `jewel`, a critical data `hinge`, for a `kilogram` of evidence. He `accepts` that the tender `heartache` was gone, replaced by the resolve of an `umpire` making a definitive call. The `hockey` `kicker` of justice, though slow, would prevail. He leaves the `weirdo` `granny` and her `bagpipe` behind, knowing his `fortunes` were forever changed, forever free from the `dorkiness` of his past. He now carries an `olive` branch of peace, no longer defined by the `prickly` past, but by the healthy future he helped to create.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone, gaunt historian, wearing a patched tweed jacket and a pair of oversized, wire-rimmed glasses, clutching a worn leatherbound journal. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A low-angle shot of the historian looking up at a vast, crumbling industrial refinery, its circuits glowing dimly, against a sunset sky. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A close-up, Dutch-angle shot of the historian's face, a look of determined apprehension, as he types furiously on a futuristic, transparent tablet, a complex firewall graphic visible on the screen. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: An eye-level, medium shot of the historian, now wearing a high-tech tactical suit, wielding a compact flamethrower, facing off against a shadowy, tribalistic figure with glowing red eyes in a dank, neon-lit underground chamber, an umbrella discarded nearby. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: A wide shot of the historian walking away from a massive, futuristic warship exploding in the distance, a small, glowing data jewel held triumphantly in his hand, a sense of relief and quiet victory on his face, cinematic lighting, bold ink outlines, NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue.

definitions.definition: Enjoying health and vigor of body, mind, or spirit: well.
definitions.example: He was father to three healthy kids.

definitions.definition: Conducive to health.
definitions.example: A healthy diet and exercise can help to maintain proper weight.

definitions.definition: Evincing health.
Significant, hefty; beneficial.
definitions.example: Sam unwrapped the sandwich and took a healthy bite out of the middle.

definitions.definition: A writer of history; a chronicler; an annalist.
One who studies or researches history.
One who recounts their own medical history.
To obtain the services of in return for fixed payment.
definitions.example: We hired a car for two weeks because ours had broken down.

definitions.definition: To employ; to obtain the services of (a person) in exchange for remuneration; to give someone a job.
definitions.example: The company had problems when it tried to hire more skilled workers.

definitions.definition: To exchange the services of for remuneration.
definitions.example: They hired themselves out as day laborers.  They hired out their basement for Inauguration week.

definitions.definition: To accomplish by paying for services.
definitions.example: After waiting two years for her husband to finish the tiling, she decided to hire it done.

definitions.definition: To accept employment.
definitions.example: They hired out as day laborers.

definitions.definition: A servant or valet to an army officer.
(by extension) A personal assistant or supporter.
To act as a batman.
A fire, especially a fast-burning fire producing a lot of flames and light.
Intense, direct light accompanied with heat.
definitions.example: to seek shelter from the blaze of the sun

definitions.definition: The white or lighter-coloured markings on a horse's face.
definitions.example: The palomino had a white blaze on its face.

definitions.definition: A high-visibility orange colour, typically used in warning signs and hunters' clothing.
A bursting out, or active display of any quality; an outburst.
A spot made on trees by chipping off a piece of the bark, usually as a surveyor's mark.
A hand consisting of five face cards.
Cloth-covered frame used for protection against rain or sun.
definitions.example: Quick, grab that umbrella before you get rained on!

definitions.definition: Generally, anything that provides protection.
definitions.example: The fighters provide a defensive air umbrella over the battle group.

definitions.definition: Something that covers a wide range of concepts, purposes, groups, etc.
definitions.example: The test facility was established under the umbrella of the company's quality program.

definitions.definition: The main body of a jellyfish, excluding the tentacles.
definitions.example: Jellyfish are composed of more than 90% water and most of their umbrella mass is made up of gelatinous material.

definitions.definition: An umbrella-shaped reflector with a white or silvery inner surface, used to diffuse a nearby light.
To cover or protect, as if by an umbrella.
To form the dome shape of an open umbrella.
To move like a jellyfish.

Any of certain marine bivalve mollusks, especially those of the family Ostreidae (the true oysters), usually found adhering to rocks or other fixed objects in shallow water along the seacoasts, or in brackish water in the mouth of rivers.
The delicate morsel of dark meat contained in a small cavity of the bone on each side of the lower part of the back of a fowl.
A pale beige color tinted with grey or pink, like that of an oyster.
(by analogy) A person who keeps secrets.
A shoplifter.
An instance of someone being killed.
(usually as make a killing) A large amount of money.
definitions.example: He made a killing on the stock market.

definitions.definition: Care, kind concern, regard.
The inner flight muscle (pectoralis minor) of poultry.
Sensitive or painful to the touch.
Easily bruised or injured; not firm or hard; delicate.
definitions.example: tender plants; tender flesh; tender fruit

definitions.definition: Physically weak; not able to endure hardship.
(of food) Soft and easily chewed.
Sensible to impression and pain; easily pained.
Fond, loving, gentle, sweet.
definitions.example: Suzanne was such a tender mother to her children.

definitions.definition: Young and inexperienced.
Adapted to excite feeling or sympathy; expressive of the softer passions; pathetic.
definitions.example: tender expressions; tender expostulations; a tender strain

definitions.definition: Apt to give pain; causing grief or pain; delicate.
definitions.example: a tender subject

definitions.definition: Heeling over too easily when under sail; said of a vessel.
Exciting kind concern; dear; precious.
Careful to keep inviolate, or not to injure; used with of.
The crime of deliberately killing another person without justification.
definitions.example: The defendant was charged with murder.

definitions.definition: The act of deliberate killing of another person or other being without justification, especially with malice aforethought.
definitions.example: There have been ten unsolved murders this year alone.

definitions.definition: (in jurisdictions which use the felony murder rule) The commission of an act which abets the commission of a crime the commission of which causes the death of a human.
(used as a predicative noun) Something terrible to endure.
definitions.example: This headache is murder.

definitions.definition: (collective) A group of crows; the collective noun for crows.
To deliberately kill (a person or persons) without justification, especially with malice aforethought.
definitions.example: The woman found dead in her kitchen was murdered by her husband.

definitions.definition: To defeat decisively.
definitions.example: Our team is going to murder them.

definitions.definition: To kick someone's ass or chew someone out (used to express one’s anger at somebody).
definitions.example: He's torn my best shirt. When I see him, I'll murder him!

definitions.definition: To botch or mangle.
To devour, ravish.
definitions.example: I could murder a hamburger right now.

definitions.definition: A non-academic periodical publication, generally consisting of sheets of paper folded in half and stapled at the fold.
An ammunition storehouse.
A chamber in a firearm enabling multiple rounds of ammunition to be fed into the firearm.
A reservoir or supply chamber for a stove, battery, camera, typesetting machine, or other apparatus.
A country or district especially rich in natural products.
A city viewed as a marketing center.
A store, or shop, where goods are kept for sale.
A fireproof barrier used to prevent the spread of fire between or through buildings, structures, electrical substation transformers, or within an aircraft or vehicle.
The software that monitors traffic in and out of a private network or a personal computer and allows or blocks such traffic depending on its perceived threat.
A hypothetical phenomenon where an observer falling into a black hole encounters high-energy quanta at or near the event horizon.
To protect with a firewall.
To block with a firewall.
(motor vehicles or aircraft) To use maximum acceleration.
To rise from a lying or sitting position.
definitions.example: Stand up, then sit down again.

definitions.definition: To bring something up and set it into a standing position.
definitions.example: Laura stood the sofa up on end.

definitions.definition: (stand someone up) To avoid a prearranged meeting, especially a date, with (a person) without prior notification; to jilt or shirk.
definitions.example: John stood Laura up at the movie theater.

definitions.definition: (of a thing) To last or endure over a period of time.
(of a person or narrative) To continue to be believable, consistent, or plausible.
(of a wicket-keeper) To stand immediately behind the wicket so as to catch balls from a slow or spin bowler, and to attempt to stump the batsman.
To launch, propel upwards
To formally activate and commission (a unit, formation, etc.).
To make one's voice heard, to speak up
To receive, especially with a consent, with favour, or with approval.
To admit to a place or a group.
definitions.example: The Boy Scouts were going to accept him as a member.

definitions.definition: To regard as proper, usual, true, or to believe in.
definitions.example: I accept the notion that Christ lived.

definitions.definition: To receive as adequate or satisfactory.
To receive or admit to; to agree to; to assent to; to submit to.
definitions.example: I accept your proposal, amendment, or excuse.

definitions.definition: To endure patiently.
definitions.example: I accept my punishment.

definitions.definition: To agree to pay.
To receive officially.
definitions.example: to accept the report of a committee

definitions.definition: To receive something willingly.
definitions.example: I accept.

definitions.definition: A substance used to treat an illness, relieve a symptom, or modify a chemical process in the body for a specific purpose.
definitions.example: Aspirin is a drug that reduces pain, acts against inflammation and lowers body temperature.

definitions.definition: A psychoactive substance, especially one which is illegal and addictive, ingested for recreational use, such as cocaine.
Anything, such as a substance, emotion or action, to which one is addicted.
definitions.example: 2010, Kesha Rose Sebert (Ke$ha), with Pebe Sebert and Joshua Coleman (Ammo), Your Love is My Drug

definitions.definition: Any commodity that lies on hand, or is not salable; an article of slow sale, or in no demand.
To administer intoxicating drugs to, generally without the recipient's knowledge or consent.
definitions.example: She suddenly felt strange, and only then realized she'd been drugged.

definitions.definition: To add intoxicating drugs to with the intention of drugging someone.
definitions.example: She suddenly felt strange. She realized her drink must have been drugged.

definitions.definition: To prescribe or administer drugs or medicines.
A drudge.
Methylated spirits mixed with red wine or Condy's crystals.
A baby mouse, especially when used as food for a snake, etc.
A white person.
The Australasian snapper or pink snapper (Chrysophrys auratus).
Pinkish.
A drug that numbs the pain in the body.
A person or group of people constituting a particular side in a contract or legal action.
definitions.example: The contract requires that the party of the first part pay the fee.

definitions.definition: A person.
A group of people forming one side in a given dispute, contest etc.
A political group considered as a formal whole, united under one specific political platform of issues and campaigning to take part in government.
definitions.example: The green party took 12% of the vote.

definitions.definition: A discrete detachment of troops, especially for a particular purpose.
definitions.example: The settlers were attacked early next morning by a scouting party.

definitions.definition: A group of persons collected or gathered together for some particular purpose.
A part or division.
To celebrate at a party, to have fun, to enjoy oneself.
definitions.example: We partied until the early hours.

definitions.definition: To take recreational drugs.
To engage in flings, to have one-night stands, to sow one's wild oats.
To form a party (with).
definitions.example: If you want to beat that monster, you should party with a healer.

definitions.definition: A trader, particularly in furs, who worked (and explored) in the area of Canada and the northern United States from the 16th to early 19th centuries; they were often of Quebecois extraction.
A building, or a mass of machinery, used to produce refined products such as sugar, oil, or metals.
To withdraw from a problematic situation.
definitions.example: Company lawyers told him to walk away from the deal.

definitions.definition: To free oneself from a debt such as a mortgage by abandoning the collateral to the lender. To make a strategic default.
To survive a challenging or dangerous situation without harm.
definitions.example: The football team walked away with a 1-0 victory.

definitions.definition: A female attendant who serves customers in a restaurant, café, or similar.
To work as a waitress.
Very wealthy and comfortable surroundings.
Something desirable but expensive.
Something that is pleasant but not necessary in life.
Very expensive
Not essential but desirable and enjoyable and indulgent.
Pertaining to the top-end market segment for mass production mass market vehicles, above the premium market segment.
The metric unit of fluid measure, equal to one cubic decimetre. Symbols: l, L, ℓ
definitions.example: You should be able to fill four cups with one litre of water.

definitions.definition: A measure of volume equivalent to a litre.
A small or indistinct shaft or stream of light.
A glimpse or hint; an indistinct sign of something.
definitions.example: The rescue workers preserved a gleam of optimism that they might still survive.

definitions.definition: Brightness or shininess; splendor.
To shine; to glitter; to glisten.
To be briefly but strongly apparent.
To disgorge filth, as a hawk.
To provide an acceptable explanation for.
definitions.example: How can you justify spending so much money on clothes?

definitions.definition: To be a good, acceptable reason for; warrant.
definitions.example: Nothing can justify your rude behaviour last night.

definitions.definition: To arrange (text) on a page or a computer screen such that the left and right ends of all lines within paragraphs are aligned.
definitions.example: The text will look better justified.

definitions.definition: To absolve, and declare to be free of blame or sin.
To give reasons for one’s actions; to make an argument to prove that one is in the right.
definitions.example: She felt no need to justify herself for deciding not to invite him.

definitions.definition: To prove; to ratify; to confirm.
To show (a person) to have had a sufficient legal reason for an act that has been made the subject of a charge or accusation.
To qualify (oneself) as a surety by taking oath to the ownership of sufficient property.
Someone who is from the hills; especially from a rural area, with a connotation of a lack of refinement or sophistication.
A white person from the rural southern part of the United States, especially the Southeastern states.
To emphasize one's rural or humble upbringing; to use unsophisticated charm.
To portray or act as an uneducated and unsophisticated fool.
To perform or experience stereotypically hillbilly-like actions.
Very sincere and difficult emotional problems or stress
A bruise-like mark made during petting by pressing the mouth to the skin on one’s partner’s body and sucking.
An object whose name is unknown or cannot be recalled.

A printing defect caused by foreign matter on the printing surface resulting in a ring where the ink is missing, appearing as a spot of ink surrounded by a halo, or as an unprinted spot within a solid printed area.
A serif or other ornamentation on type.
Local swelling in a tissue area, especially skin, often due to injury.
A tool for making smooth, semicircular bends in conduit and pipe.
An unintended negative outcome or loss in regards to a deal or action, often preceded by the words "taking a ..." or "took a ... ".
definitions.example: Farmer Jones took a hickey on his soybean crop when the flood washed the seeds away.

definitions.definition: An activity that one enjoys doing in one's spare time.
definitions.example: I like to collect stamps from different countries as a hobby.

definitions.definition: An extinct breed of horse native to the British Isles, also known as the Irish Hobby
The width, usually measured in hertz, of a frequency band.
Of a signal, the width of the smallest frequency band within which the signal can fit.
The rate of data flow in digital networks typically measured in bits per second; the bitrate.
The capacity, energy or time required.
definitions.example: I think it's a worthy project, but I just don't have the bandwidth right now.

definitions.definition: An Okinawan martial art involving primarily punching and kicking, but additionally, advanced throws, arm bars, grappling and all means of fighting.
To attack (somebody or something) with karate or similar techniques.
A device that projects a flame for starting fires, and sometimes also additional fuel to help ignition. Used either as a weapon or a tool.
A final examination; a test or examination given at the end of a term or class; the test that concludes a class.
The last round, game or match in a contest, after which the winner is determined.

A contest that narrows a field of contestants (finalists) to ranked positions, usually in numbered places (1st place/prize, 2nd place/prize, etc.) or a winner and numbered runners-up (1st runner-up, etc.).
The final part of a syllable, the combination of medial and rime in phonetics and phonology.
The tonic or keynote of a Gregorian mode, and hence the final note of any conventional melody played in that mode.
Last; ultimate.
definitions.example: final solution;   the final day of a school term

definitions.definition: Conclusive; decisive.
definitions.example: a final judgment;   the battle of Waterloo brought the contest to a final issue

definitions.definition: Respecting an end or object to be gained; respecting the purpose or ultimate end in view.
(grammar) Expressing purpose; as in the term final clause.
Word-final, occurring at the end of a word.
To become conscious after having slept.
To cause (somebody) to stop sleeping.
To excite or to stir up something latent.
To rouse from a state of inaction or dormancy.
To come out of a state of inaction or dormancy.
To cause to become awake.
definitions.example: She awakened him by ringing the bell.

definitions.definition: To stop sleeping; awake.
definitions.example: Each morning he awakens with a smile on his face.

definitions.definition: To bring into action (something previously dormant); to stimulate.
definitions.example: Awaken your entrepreneurial spirit!

definitions.definition: To call to a sense of sin.
To fall apart; to disintegrate.
definitions.example: The empire crumbled when the ruler's indiscretions came to light.

definitions.definition: To break into crumbs.
definitions.example: We crumbled some bread into the water.

definitions.definition: To mix (ingredients such as flour and butter) in such a way as to form crumbs.
definitions.example: Using your fingers, crumble the ingredients with the fingertips, lifting in an upward motion, until the mixture is sandy and resembles large breadcrumbs.

definitions.definition: Material that has crumbled away; crumbs.
The ability to hold, receive or absorb
A measure of such ability; volume
The maximum amount that can be held
definitions.example: It was hauling a capacity load.

definitions.definition: Capability; the ability to perform some task
The maximum that can be produced.
Mental ability; the power to learn
A faculty; the potential for growth and development
A role; the position in which one functions
Legal authority (to make an arrest for example)
Electrical capacitance.
(operations) The maximum that can be produced on a machine or in a facility or group.
definitions.example: Its capacity rating was 150 tons per hour, but its actual maximum capacity was 200 tons per hour.

definitions.definition: Filling the allotted space.
definitions.example: There will be a capacity crowd at Busch stadium for the sixth game.

definitions.definition: The state of being dark; lack of light.
definitions.example: The darkness of the room made it difficult to see.

definitions.definition: Gloom.
The product of being dark.
The state or quality of reflecting little light, of tending to a blackish or brownish color.
definitions.example: The darkness of her skin betrayed her Mediterranean heritage.

definitions.definition: Evilness, lack of understanding or compassion, reference to death or suffering.
Something that gives a pricking sensation; a sharp object.
Covered with sharp points.
definitions.example: The prickly pear is a cactus; you have to peel it before eating it to remove the spines and the tough skin.

definitions.definition: Easily irritated.
definitions.example: He has a prickly personality. He doesn't get along with people because he is easily set off.

definitions.definition: Difficult; complicated; (figuratively) hairy or thorny.
definitions.example: It was a prickly situation.

definitions.definition: In a prickly manner.
To graciously give, to condescendingly grant a right, benefit, outcome, etc.; to deign to acknowledge.
To receive or accept in condescension.
To disclose or divulge.
definitions.example: She vouchsafed to me that she regretted ever marrying him.

definitions.definition: A mythological undead creature said to feed on the blood of the living.
A person with the medical condition systemic lupus erythematosus, colloquially known as vampirism, with effects such as photosensitivity and brownish-red stained teeth.
A blood-sucking bat; vampire bat (Desmodus rotundus)
A person who drains one's time, energy, money, etc.
A vamp: a seductive woman who exploits men.
A medical technician who works with patients' blood.
To drain of energy or resources.

An activity designed to give pleasure, enjoyment, diversion, amusement, or relaxation to an audience, no matter whether the audience participates passively as in watching opera or a movie, or actively as in games.
A show put on for the enjoyment or amusement of others.
Maintenance or support.
Admission into service; service.

definitions.definition: A barrier.
definitions.example: Keep a door on your anger.

definitions.definition: A software mechanism by which a user can interact with a program running remotely on a bulletin board system. See BBS door.
To cause a collision by opening the door of a vehicle in front of an oncoming cyclist or pedestrian.
A polygon with five sides and five angles.
A fort with five bastions.
A shaking of the ground, caused by volcanic activity or movement around geologic faults.
(planetary geology) Such a quake specifically occurring on the planet Earth, as opposed to other celestial bodies.
The act of enchanting or the feeling of being enchanted.
Something that enchants; a magical spell.
No particular place, noplace.
definitions.example: They went on a cruise to nowhere.

definitions.definition: Unimportant; unworthy of notice.
In no place.
definitions.example: Nowhere did the rules say anything about popcorn.

definitions.definition: To no place.
definitions.example: We sat in traffic, going nowhere.
